Narrative_Meaning: Queens signify an older female, matriarchy, scandals, social events, curiosity, love, wisdom, sinister manipulations and betrayal.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Inn card represents the den of iniquity, impropriety, sins and vices that every society has. The Inn may simply be the place that exotic outsiders stay in town, but more often it is the place of shady deals, criminal and immoral activities and recreational intoxicants. It offers satisfaction, comfort, protection, anonymity, and a certain lack of consequences to those who visit, with hints of adventure, rebelliousness, risk, and even danger for those that seek it.

Significator.Plot: The Inn Plot is about sins, dirty secrets and adult situations. There is always an air of adventure to the Inn, a temptation that can be a part of coming of age plots, and such like, but is more normally a part of a deeper awakening.
Significator.Side: The side of a conflict marked by the Inn will have a good time during the plot, they may party, dance, love, drink, eat and generally be merry, sure there may be drama, fights, even a duel or two, but they will survive and enjoy themselves anyway, usually.
Significator.Character:
The Inn Significator indicates a Character who revels in the joys of life.

They Gain Yin when they take pleasure in something.

They Gain Yang when they tempt others.

They Gain Chi when they enjoy something socially.

They also gain +1 Success Level on all passive defences.
Significator.Scene: The Inn Scene is usually a Character's first taste of a wider world. There are strange folk in the Inn, some look weird, some speak weird, some just are weird and a few want weird things. A Yarn-Teller can play the Inn Scene to secure themselves a place to stay, or play, or a chance to meet new people.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with a stranger arriving in town. This person may be a Rival or an Embodiment, but more normally is some unknown Ally who brings the PCs some warning or message about a threat beyond the town.
Yarn.Fray_Description: The Fray involves a complex social Ordeal, usually of Medium Stakes, although higher and lower Stakes versions are possible. There will be drinks, usually alcoholic, which may be replaced or augmented by other recreational drugs. This Fray is social and allows Characters to alter their social status with other Characters, earning respect, friendship or enmity as appropriate.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Something about the situation is either Illegal or Immoral, ideally it should be a choice between immoral or illegal (such as keeping slaves being immoral, but freeing slaves being illegal).

NPC.Description: The Bar-tender is a common archetype, you'll find one behind most bars. They enjoy meeting people and don't mind hearing another's problems and offering them beverages. May sometimes cross over with Priests and Cleric types, who also practice listening to problems and offering beverages.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs may have cause to celebrate from recent events, more likely they want to distract themselves from recent events with a celebration. There will be drinks, there will be music or games, and there is usually food (often cake). It may be a Character's birthday, or a similar anniversary, or a feast or festival.
Yarn.History_Description: The Inn indicates that in the history of the current situation there was a visitation, someone came, or went and visited somewhere. This visit may have been a vacation, a business trip, research, some family emergency, or may even have supernatural or alien connotations.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Inn indicates that the current situation is one where either you find yourself receiving guests, or you are a guest. This situation can be pleasant or stressful as you may be unfamiliar with each other, when there may be culture shocks on both sides, or you may be too familiar with each other, which can create its own stresses. Hospitality always has its limits, although different lines will be drawn in different cultures.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Inn indicates that the solution to the current situation lies in the immoral. Sometimes this may mean contacting the "criminal underworld" to make illegal purchases, other times it means not making it home that night.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about a pleasure that is available. This might be a particular food, drug, or experience unique to the Location or Story, or could be information about some common pleasure.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about what an Embodiment likes to do with their time is always useful. Sometimes we can learn more from someone's Vices than from their words.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Adverts are everywhere in our society and always conveying some information to us. But this one is different, maybe its a private ad, simply saying "Safety not guaranteed", or perhaps it's the yells of a street vendor, telling us of distant lands and the quality of their silks and spices. Then again news vendors yelling out "click-bait" headlines is as old as printing.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information covers the reasons the bad guy is doing what he is doing and it turns out that, yeah, he might be doing the right thing after all. Maybe he is leading an army invading the country because his twin sister has been kidnapped by the king, or perhaps the reason he butchers the desert snake tribes that way is because he was raised by a nest of serpent people hiding in the capital city, after they slaughtered his family in front of him.
Revelations.Detail_Description: A Mistake detail could be a misspelled word, or an auto-corrected text, or it could be a deliberate mistake. So are the outlaws you're tracking inside the barn and forgot to post a rear guard, or are they well aware you are out here and have deliberately left a way in?

Obstacle.Description: The Obstacle is a temptation, traditionally this is represented by a Pleasure Dome filled with loads of semi-naked, sexually-alluring people, but temptations vary, drink (a bar), gambling (casino), drugs (opium den / crack-house), money or valuables lying around, or even a video game may also qualify.

Tarot.Normal: +1 Success Level to everyone following the Plan while atop pile. Additionally creates a "Plan" Reserve for the team, that they may each Prepare one card from their Hand to, as an Ordeal Card. The Plan Reserve may be used for any Planned action. The Reserve is Discarded when the Plan is covered or Discarded.
Tarot.Crossed: +2 Success Levels when working alone while atop pile. When played on a Target their Hand is Prepared as Ordeal Cards into a "Plan" Reserve, and they redraw they Wyrd Tarot Hand. The Reserve is Discarded when the Plan is covered or Discarded.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 2 Obstacles in every Non-Court Stage, 3 in every Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: A number of Obstacles, A trap/or other Obstacles, an unforeseen expense
Ordeal.Motional_Description: He blows you a kiss, and steps back from the edge of the building, dropping from sight, before he unfurls his wings and slips into a long glide across the rooftops. You hurl your grappling hook, and swing to chase.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Most of the Ebbing side are in poor positions, and either need a ploy to create a distraction to allow them to redeploy, or need to be used as a distraction while a ploy is attempted. If the Ebbing side can manage to play a Trick this Stage then they will turn the Tide and the battle will start Flowing for them, failure to play a Trick will result in defeat.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The fight is against a solitary opponent who is around the level of the strongest PC. Usually the opponent will use subterfuge and asymmetrical warfare to isolate and neutralise the PCs. Usually a Monster with at least one ♣ Monster Facet.
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or Dragon configurations.

Stress.Description: Stress can make monsters of us all, but worse still Stress can make Monsters, not just mountains, out of molehills.
Stress.Rules: The Character must pay Chi equal to the Character’s current Stress as Yarn. This Chi is used to purchase a new Character that can be a Force-of-Nature Extra with Monster Facets (Burden, Dominion, Fury, Rook, Sin, or Wyrd). These "Monsters" may actually be physical issues such as natural disasters, or they may be aspects of the Characters own Psyche, given hallucinatory or supernatural form. Then again they can be the Character themselves, unaware in their Stress induced black-outs what they are doing. Other times the Character will project their problems on an actual Monster, tying their Arcs together. The only catharsis available to a Character here is to defeat, or at least survive the Monster, which will relieve Stress up to Monster Facet Boon.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can create all sorts of problems for Characters, and can place them in situations where they discover things about themselves that even they never suspected. Sometimes the trauma can be directly responsible for a fetish, addiction, or mania for something, othertimes it may be a reaction to the trauma that leads a Character "astray". Medical treatments, as well as recreational activities can become addicting, or problematical, temptations come from many sources, and new temptations are all the more tempting.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma grants the Character the ability to Relieve 1 Stress by indulging in a temptation, however they can Relieve all current Stress if they indulge in a new temptation. New temptations can, of course, be dangerous as well as tempting, with Stakes ranging from Medium to Paradoxical depending upon the genre and temptation.

Age.Description: An Age of Decadence is occurring. During this age society revels in immorality, iniquity, sinfulness and wrongdoing, rather than just the excesses of wealth. Although the wealthy will display impropriety too, extravagant debaucheries that rival those of an age of ostentation, for the underclasses of society there is just as much excess in some ways, but it often involves stronger or at least cheaper booze.
Age.Rules: During the Age of Decadence most members of society will develop at least one additional Hitch that reflects the dark hobbies they are developing during the age.
